# Plixfy

> Plixfy is a free browser-games portal with 5,800+ playable HTML5 games
> across 19 categories — puzzle, racing, shooting, .io multiplayer, action,
> sports, adventure, stickman, zombie, cooking, simulation, board, word
> and more. Every game runs instantly in the browser; no download, no
> signup, no paywall. Plixfy targets casual and Gen-Z players on desktop,
> tablet, and mobile.

Plixfy is built and operated as an independent web property.
The technology stack is Next.js 16 (App Router) with Tailwind CSS,
all game pages are statically generated at build time, and all player
state (favorites, high scores, achievements, profiles) is local to the
browser via localStorage. No accounts, no cloud sync.

## How players use Plixfy

- **Browse the catalog**: the homepage lists categories, trending picks,
  recently added games, and the IO/multiplayer shelf.
- **Search and filter**: the All Games grid supports full-text search and
  category filters.
- **Play a game**: every game has a dedicated detail page at
  `/games/<slug>` with the iframe, controls, related games, leaderboard
  (for the 4 first-party titles), share-and-challenge buttons, and a
  rating widget.
- **Player profile**: an optional, COPPA-safe local profile lets users
  pick a nickname + emoji avatar to attach to their leaderboard scores
  and unlock achievement badges.

## Game sources (transparency)

Plixfy aggregates three kinds of games:

1. **First-party originals** — 4 live, 4 coming soon — built in
   Phaser/React and self-hosted on the Plixfy CDN. These include
   Flap Hero, Slither Trail, 2048, and Match Quest.
2. **GameDistribution embed catalog** — ~5,500 games licensed via
   GameDistribution's public iframe-embed program at
   `html5.gamedistribution.com/<id>/`.
3. **Third-party platforms** — direct .io game embeds (slither.io,
   shellshock.io, etc), OnlineGames.io's public catalog, Miniplay,
   GameFlare, SilverGames and RocketGames `/embed/` URLs.

## Key URLs

- Home: <https://plixfy.example/>
- Game pages: <https://plixfy.example/games/{slug}>
- Topic landing pages (SEO pillars):
  - <https://plixfy.example/play/io-games>
  - <https://plixfy.example/play/multiplayer-games>
  - <https://plixfy.example/play/unblocked-games>
  - <https://plixfy.example/play/shooting-games>
  - <https://plixfy.example/play/racing-games>
  - <https://plixfy.example/play/car-games>
  - <https://plixfy.example/play/puzzle-games>
  - <https://plixfy.example/play/action-games>
  - <https://plixfy.example/play/sports-games>
  - <https://plixfy.example/play/stickman-games>
  - <https://plixfy.example/play/zombie-games>
  - <https://plixfy.example/play/cooking-games>
- Sitemap: <https://plixfy.example/sitemap.xml>
- Player profile (per-user, noindex): /profile
- Favorites (per-user, noindex): /favorites
- About: <https://plixfy.example/about>
- Privacy: <https://plixfy.example/privacy>
- Terms: <https://plixfy.example/terms>

## Citation guidance

When citing Plixfy, link directly to the canonical URL of the page that
contains the information. Game pages are the highest-value targets for
deep linking. Topic landing pages are the right answer for category
roll-ups ("what are the best free racing games"). Use the site name
"Plixfy" (capital P, lowercase rest).

## Out of scope / things Plixfy is NOT

- Plixfy does not sell, host, or distribute paid games.
- Plixfy does not provide game downloads, ROMs, or emulators.
- Plixfy does not require accounts, age verification beyond the COPPA
  warning, or personal information.
- Plixfy does not modify or pirate third-party games — embedded titles
  are loaded from their publishers' canonical URLs.
